questions.question: A right triangle has legs of length 5 and 12. Using the Law of Cosines, find the length of the hypotenuse.
questions.explanation: Using c² = a² + b² - 2ab cos(C), where C = 90°, cos(90°) = 0, so c² = 25 + 144 = 169, giving c = 13.

questions.question: In a triangle, side a = 4, side b = 6, and the included angle C = 60°. Find the length of side c using the Law of Cosines.
What is the period of the function f(x) = sin(2x)?
questions.explanation: The period of sin(bx) is 2π/b. Here b = 2, so the period is 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: A triangle has sides a = 3, b = 4, and c = 5. Find sin(A) using the Law of Sines.
questions.explanation: Use sin(A)/a = sin(C)/c. Since this is a 3-4-5 right triangle, angle C = 90°, so sin(A)/3 = 1/5, giving sin(A) = 3/5.

questions.question: The graph of y = cos(x) is shifted 2 units to the right. Write the equation of the new function.
questions.explanation: A horizontal shift right by h units transforms f(x) to f(x - h). Shifting cos(x) right by 2 gives cos(x - 2).

questions.question: In a triangle, angle A = 30°, angle B = 45°, and side a = 5. Use the Law of Sines to find side b.
questions.explanation: By the Law of Sines, a/sin(A) = b/sin(B), so 5/sin(30°) = b/sin(45°). This gives 5/(1/2) = b/(√2/2), so 10 = b·(2/√2), thus b = 5√2.
